title: Topology-Variant Group Contract
description:
Declares the catalog's topology-variant groups: sets of components that are mutually-exclusive deployment topologies of the SAME product (a consumer picks exactly one member), where a concrete requires: pin to any member is satisfiable by any member of the group. Lives as the single central file catalog/topology-groups.yaml and is validated by task validate:topology-groups (in task ci). It does NOT change compatibility.schema.json, catalog/capability-index.yaml, any component compatibility.yaml, or any consumer requires: key — the requires: key stays a concrete <sub-layer>/<component> so catalog/README.md's concrete-pin rule is unweakened.

IMPORTANT — expressibility only, NOT drop-in runtime wiring: the runtime write-endpoint DIFFERS between topologies (e.g. alloy must target loki-distributed-distributor, not loki). 'either satisfies' is a contract-expressibility property; swapping topologies still requires a consumer-overlay endpoint change (ADR-0024). Same-product-ness is REVIEW-enforced and necessary-not-sufficient (no sound mechanical oracle exists — capability-set identity false-accepts genuine tool-swaps and empty sets); see knowledge/decisions/DR-0003-topology-variant-contract.md.

Schema-contract-parity decisions (rules/schema-contract-parity.md):
1. Closed-vs-open: closed via additionalProperties:false at the top level and on each group object — parse hygiene plus a reject-legacy-key threat model (an unknown key fails validation), mirroring compatibility.schema.json decision 1. The gate extracts only the documented fields.
2. Duplicate-key behavior: duplicate top-level or per-group YAML keys are a YAML authoring error; readers treat the file as corrupt and surface it, not silently last-wins (identical to compatibility.schema.json decision 2). Group id uniqueness cannot be keyed by a JSON-Schema uniqueItems, so the gate (validate:topology-groups) enforces it separately — a duplicate id fails task ci.
3. No schema version field — evolution tracked via git history under harness-evolution 2-round review (rules/review-rigor.md). Migration path: a new kind enum value (e.g. a future role-additive) is added under that review with the gate + fixtures extended in the SAME PR; readers detect change via the schema/gate diff.
4. catalog/topology-groups.yaml is repo-internal trusted data — no untrusted-data sentinel applies (identical to compatibility.schema.json decision 4).
5. Per-field mutability: groups[] is append-only at the group level (a group is a stable consumer-facing contract; removing one is a breaking, consumer-coordinated change). members[] is append-only within a group. default is mutable-in-place but stable-by-convention (fixed when the component is first introduced, AGENTS.md Topology variants); a change is a breaking, consumer-coordinated event, and its leaf name is gate-checked to equal the group id. kind is mutable-in-place structurally but effectively fixed (one enum value today).

groups.description: The topology-variant groups. Each group is a set of mutually-exclusive topology alternatives of one product.

id.description: Stable group identifier. By gate convention it equals the bare (default) component name and is unique across all groups (validate:topology-groups enforces both).

kind.description: The relationship the group asserts. 'mutual-exclusion' = the members are alternative topologies of the same product; a consumer deploys exactly one. This is author-declared and review-enforced; there is no mechanical same-product oracle (see DR-0003). A role-additive split (e.g. the alloy / alloy-metrics / alloy-singleton trio) is NOT a topology group and must not be declared here.

default.description: The product's default/primary topology, a member of members[]. Its leaf name (after the last '/') MUST equal the group id (gate-checked), preserving the AGENTS.md bare-name-default convention. Stable-by-convention; a change is a breaking, consumer-coordinated event.

members.description: The mutually-exclusive member components, each a concrete <sub-layer>/<component> id that maps to an existing sub-layers/<sub-layer>/components/<component>/ directory (gate-checked). A concrete requires: pin to any member is satisfied by any member.
